name: Dave's Ribs
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup sugar (run through coffee grinder to make TURBINADO)
2 tablespoons Kosher or sea salt (run through coffee grinder with sugar)
recipeInstructions:
The night before...
1. Trim the ribs of all hard fat and remove the membrane from the underside by poking a hole, running your finger under it, and ripping it away.
2. Rub both sides of the ribs with a thick coat of the mixture: ground black pepper, paprika, sugar (TURBINADO), Kosher or sea salt, dry mustard, and cayenne pepper.
3. Place the ribs in a plastic bag and refrigerate overnight.
The next day:
4. Remove the ribs from the refrigerator and let them reach room temperature.
5. Start a fire in your Webber Grill or smoker with charcoal. As the coals turn gray, add hard wood chunks or water-soaked wood chips.
6. Bring the smoker temperature to between 210 and 225 degrees by adjusting the vents. Maintain this temperature throughout the smoking (90 minutes) by adding more wood or charcoal.
7. Place the ribs on the rack and smoke cook them in heavy smoke for at least two hours, adding wood or chips every 30 minutes. The ribs should have a dark "crust" formed by the smoke clinging to the rub.
8. Preheat the oven to 225 degrees. Place the ribs on racks over cookie sheets and bake for three hours, or until the rib bones are easily removed when twisted (dry ribs). No extra spices or mop were used in the cooking process.
9. To make juicier ribs, brush them with barbecue sauce and cover with aluminum foil to bake for the last hour.
10. Ribs can be made up to two days ahead and reheated in a warm oven.
Serve with barbecue beans, coleslaw, potato salad, and plenty of cold beer. Enjoy!
